      • `$`(value: Value): string

      • initValue(procId: int; procScript: string): Value

      • initValue(v: float64): Value
      • initValue(v: int64): Value
      • initValue(v: JsonNode): Value

        toJsonStr
      • toJsonStr(v: Value): string
        toStorage

        toString
      • toString(value: JsonNode): string
        toValue
      • toValue(vs: ValueStorage): Value

This module defines the Value type, which represents a value in the interpreter. This represents all the possible values that can be manipulated by the interpreter, including primitive types like bools and ints, as well as complex types like objects and arrays.

It also includes utilities for initializing and converting values, as well as a foreign proc system for handling native code in the standard library and user-defined foreign procs in general.

Types

ForeignData {.acyclic.} = object
  data*: pointer
  tag*: string
  destructor*: proc (data: pointer) {.nimcall.}
ForeignProc = proc (args: StackView; argc: int): Value
A foreign proc implementation, used for native code in the standard library and user-defined foreign procs in general 
JitHooks = object
  getForeign*: proc (procPtr: pointer): ForeignProc ## Given a Proc pointer, returns its JIT-compiled ForeignProc if available.
  queueCompile*: proc (theProc: pointer) ## Queue a proc for async JIT compilation.
                                         ## Called from markHotProc. theProc is cast(pointer, Proc).
  setGlobalsPtr*: proc (p: pointer) ## Set the pointer to the VM's globals CritBitTree for JIT bridge access.
  compileTrace*: proc (trace: pointer): pointer ## Compile a TraceBuffer into machine code. Returns code pointer or nil.
JIT hooks installed into the VM. Uses raw pointer to avoid circular deps between vm and jit modules. 
Object {.acyclic.} = ref object
  case isForeign*: bool
  of true:
    foreign*: ForeignData
  of false:
    keys*: seq[string]       ## the field names of this object
                             ## stored in the same order as `fields`
    fields*: seq[ValueStorage] ## the fields of this object, stored
                               ## in the same order as `keys`
Represents an object value, which can be either a native object with fields or a foreign object with a data pointer and library path 
ProcRef = object
  procId*: int
  procScript*: string
Stack = seq[Value]
A runtime stack of values, used in the VM. 
StackView = ptr UncheckedArray[Value]
An unsafe view into a Stack. 
TypeId = range[0 .. 32766]
Value {.acyclic.} = ref object
  case typeId*: TypeId       ## the type ID, used for dynamic dispatch
  of tyBool:
    boolVal*: bool
  of tyInt:
    intVal*: int64
  of tyFloat:
    floatVal*: float64
  of tyString:
    stringVal*: ref string
  of tyJsonStorage:
    jsonVal*: JsonNode
  of tyProc:
    procVal*: ref ProcRef
  else:
    objectVal*: Object
ValueStorage = object
  case typeId*: TypeId
  of tyInt:
    intVal*: int64
  of tyBool:
    boolVal*: bool
  of tyFloat:
    floatVal*: float64
  of tyNil:
    nil
  else:
    refVal*: Value

Consts

nilObject = -1
The field count used for initializing a nil object. 
tyAny = 16
tyArrayObject = 12
tyBool = 1
tyCoroutine = 17
tyFirstObject = 10
tyFloat = 3
tyInt = 2
tyJsonStorage = 11
tyNil = 0
tyPointer = 15
tyProc = 18
tyString = 4
ValueSize = 16

Procs

proc `$`(value: Value): string {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Returns a value's string representation. 
proc `=destroy`(fd: ForeignData) {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc dumpHook(s: var string; val: Value) {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect],
    forbids: [].}
OpenParser JSON dumping hook for Values 
proc foreign(value: Value; T: typedesc): T:type
Get an object value. This is a mostly safe operation, but attempting to get a foreign type different from the value's is undefined behavior. 
proc initArray(length: int): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes an array value. 
proc initObject(id: TypeId; fieldCount: int): Value {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initializes a native object value, with fieldCount fields. 
proc initValue(nptr: pointer; tag: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initializes a pointer value. 
proc initValue(procId: int; procScript: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initializes a proc reference value. 
proc initValue(v: bool): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a bool value. 
proc initValue(v: float64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a float value. 
proc initValue(v: int64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a float value. 
proc initValue(v: JsonNode): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a JSON value. 
proc initValue(v: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a string value. 
proc initValue[T: tuple | object | ref](id: TypeId; value: T): Value
Safely initializes a foreign object value. This copies the value onto the heap for ordinary objects and tuples, and GC_refs the value for refs. 
proc toJsonStr(v: Value): string {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc toStorage(v: Value): ValueStorage {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc toString(value: JsonNode): string {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Converts a JSON node to a string. 
proc toValue(vs: ValueStorage): Value {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}
